commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

evaluation.critique: The component has decent data handling and hierarchy, but significant issues with design token consistency (hardcoded fallback colors in ForecastStrip, incorrect icon usage), missing WeatherHeader implementation, and lack of interactive polish. The layout is reasonable but WeatherDetails uses inappropriate icons (user/settings instead of weather-related) and spacing could be more refined.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Category color-coding logic is inconsistent and decoupled across components; the logic in 'constants.ts' is ignored, and 'ElementCell.tsx' and 'Legend.tsx' use hardcoded switches that don't match the actual input data.
issues.fix: Use the CATEGORY_COLORS map from 'constants.ts' consistently in both the Legend and the ElementCell components.

issues.description: The search input is implemented via 'SearchField' which is not part of the provided @ggui-ai/design primitive set (usually Input component is used).
issues.fix: Replace with the standard Input component from @ggui-ai/design.

issues.description: Keyboard interaction is incomplete. While the element has a role and tabIndex, the focus outline is not explicitly managed, and there is no visible focus state for keyboard users.
issues.fix: Add a focus-visible selector to the ElementCell styles to ensure keyboard users can see which element is selected.

issues.description: The element-to-grid mapping uses hardcoded grid coordinates, but the search filter removes elements, which can cause the layout to 'break' or jump significantly.
issues.fix: Keep all cells in the grid but apply an opacity of 0.3 to non-matching elements instead of removing them from the DOM.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and functional, but suffers from data-logic fragmentation between the constants file and the component-specific color switches. Using a unified look-up map for colors across all sub-components would greatly improve maintainability and visual consistency.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The avatar selection feature (emoji picker) requested in Step 1 is entirely missing from the implementation.
issues.fix: Add an avatar selector using the Avatar or RadioGroup component in Step 1.

issues.description: Theme preference (light/dark toggle) requested in Step 2 is missing from the StepContent component.
issues.fix: Add a Toggle component to the Step 2 section to update the 'theme' field.

issues.description: The provided code is fragmented into multiple files with conflicting 'MainView' exports and incorrect import paths, making the component non-functional as a single coherent unit.
issues.fix: Consolidate into a single module or ensure clean orchestration between files with a unified entry point.

issues.description: The 'Next' button validation logic in the hook does not account for mandatory fields in Step 2 (e.g., Role selection) or avatar selection.
issues.fix: Update the isNextDisabled logic to include validation for all mandatory fields across steps.

evaluations.critique: The component structure is highly disorganized, with multiple files attempting to define the same entry point, and it fails to implement several core functional requirements like avatar selection and theme toggling. While the basic React skeleton is present, the logic for multi-step navigation and field validation is incomplete and needs significant refactoring to be production-ready.
